symptoms.symptom_name: Acute symmetric MCP/PIP/MTP polyarthritis with prolonged morning stiffness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetric MCP/PIP/MTP synovitis with >1h stiffness is the classic RA distribution.
Widespread symmetric involvement is against monoarticular joint sepsis.
Fever in an immunomodulated patient must prompt infection exclusion before immunosuppression.
Low-grade fever can accompany an RA flare, but does not exclude infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: No single hot/red joint; no high fever/rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a single fiery joint and high fever/rigors argues against septic arthritis, though examination is needed.
Viral illness is a recognised RA-flare trigger.
A viral trigger overlaps but the established RA distribution favours a flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: High fall risk from MTP pain and stiffness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Painful weight-bearing with stiffness creates a genuine fall risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetric boggy synovitis, positive squeeze test, no single hot joint (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive squeeze test with symmetric boggy synovitis fits an RA flare.
No single hot joint on exam argues against sepsis.
Established seropositive RA is the substrate for the flare.
Immunomodulation modestly raises infection risk and lowers the threshold to exclude it.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Symmetric MCP/PIP/MTP synovitis with a positive squeeze test and no single hot joint, on established seropositive RA after a viral illness, is an RA flare — but a low-grade fever in an immunomodulated patient mandates infection exclusion before steroid escalation, and the joints cannot be examined remotely; urgent in-person review plus fall-safety is required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person joint examination and infection screen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To confirm the flare pattern, exclude a septic joint/systemic infection, and guide steroid decisions; cannot be done via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods (FBC, CRP, ESR, blood cultures if febrile) ± urinalysis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess inflammation/infection in a febrile immunomodulated patient.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Joint aspiration if any single joint becomes disproportionately inflamed
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude septic arthritis if the exam raises concern.
